page: Amazon Web Services x1e.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance utilizing the Xen hypervisor. It is powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at 2.3 GHz. The configuration includes 64 virtual CPUs (32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled) and a 45 MB L3 cache. The server features 1952.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 30.5 GB of RAM per core. Local storage is provided via a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 10 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear tradeoff between single-core and multi-core capabilities. Single-core CPU performance, measured by stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 10% of tested systems, and single-threaded compression tasks are similarly weak. However, multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression align with the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth tests show average read and write speeds for large blocks, though the Passmark memory score is positioned in the bottom 25%. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput all perform within the average tier.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, this server provides high memory density per vCPU, making it suitable for memory-bound workloads rather than compute-heavy tasks. Given the massive memory capacity and local NVMe SSD storage, the instance is suited for large in-memory databases, database operations, caching layers, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM but moderate CPU performance.
description: Amazon Web Services x1e.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance designed for memory-intensive workloads. Powered by an Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor running at 2.3 GHz, it features 64 vCPUs, 1952.0 GB of RAM, and 1920 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Benchmark data reveals a significant performance tradeoff: single-core CPU execution is weak, ranking in the bottom 10% to 25%, while multi-core operations, database transactions, and Redis SET operations perform in the average tier. This hardware profile makes the instance highly suitable for large-scale in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound enterprise applications where massive RAM capacity is prioritized over high single-threaded compute performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server featuring 64 vCPUs, 1952 GB of RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD, designed for large in-memory databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services x1e.16xlarge is a memory-optimized server with 64 vCPUs, 1952 GB RAM, and a 1920 GB NVMe SSD for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized instance featuring high RAM density and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive enterprise applications and databases.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 1952 GB of RAM providing 30.5 GB of memory per core
1920 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Intel Xeon E7-8880 v3 processor with dedicated CPU allocation
Average multi-core and database performance paired with weak single-core speeds
Suited for large in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound workloads
